title: Hummus

overview.description: A smooth, creamy hummus made from chickpeas, tahini, lemon and garlic. Simple ingredients, balanced flavour and a silky texture make this a versatile accompaniment for countless meals.

ingredients: 2 x 400g tins chickpeas, drained (reserve 100ml of the liquid)
method: Drain the chickpeas, reserving about 100ml of the liquid. Rinse the chickpeas well under cold water.
Add the chickpeas, tahini, garlic, lemon juice, olive oil, cumin and salt to a food processor.
Blend for about 2 minutes until the mixture begins to smooth out, scraping down the sides of the bowl as needed.

With the processor still running, gradually add the reserved chickpea liquid followed by the ice-cold water until the hummus becomes light, smooth and creamy. The exact amount of water will depend on the chickpeas and your preferred consistency.
Taste and adjust the seasoning. A little more lemon brightens the flavour, while a little more salt often brings everything together.
Transfer to a serving bowl. Use the back of a spoon to create a shallow swirl, then drizzle with a little olive oil before serving.

learning.did-you-know: Adding ice-cold water while blending helps create a lighter, fluffier hummus. Many cooks also find it produces a creamier texture than using extra oil alone.
learning.what-you-will-learn: How to balance acidity, salt and richness, and how liquid affects the final texture of blended dips.

notes.why-its-in-the-chapman-kitchen: Uses mostly pantry ingredients, takes only minutes to make and works as part of many meals or simply as a healthy snack.
chapman-tips: Blend for longer than you think; extra blending makes a noticeable difference.
Taste before serving. Hummus often needs a final squeeze of lemon or pinch of salt.
For an extra smooth finish, peel the chickpeas if you have the time, although we usually don't bother.
notes.batch-cooking: Keeps well in the fridge for several days. Stir before serving if it thickens slightly.
notes.leftovers: Excellent in sandwiches, wraps or as a topping for baked potatoes.
